authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 484.50 - Condition of participation: Patient rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal home health agency condition of participation for patient rights, notices, complaint communication, representative handling, and privacy-sensitive support boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request intake, requester authority, complaint handling, and patient-rights-sensitive communication
used_for: customer complaint and representative boundary handling
refusal to close support cases that require privacy or agency grievance ownership

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 484.60 - Condition of participation: Care planning, coordination of services, and quality of care
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal home health condition of participation for plan-of-care orders, visit frequency, coordination of services, written patient instructions, and clinician or allowed-practitioner responsibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: visit-status review, plan-of-care boundary handling, field-service coordination, and clinical escalation
used_for: visit frequency and plan-of-care artifact expectations
refusal to change care plans or clinical instructions

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Services Coverage
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Medicare beneficiary guidance for home health coverage, care plan, HHA scheduling, what Medicare does or does not pay for, ABN notification, and Medicare Advantage benefit caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, benefit, coverage, ABN, and customer-facing coverage caveat handling
used_for: ABN and patient cost notification escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS manual for Medicare home health benefit policy, including homebound, skilled need, certification, recertification, duration, exclusions, and service-prerequisite concepts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coverage prerequisite, skilled-need, homebound, certification, and recertification dependency handling
used_for: medical necessity and payer interpretation boundary

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Minimum-necessary guidance directly constrains support notes, patient or caregiver communication, and escalation packets containing PHI.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-gated support communication and escalation packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for PHI uses and disclosures tied to treatment, payment, health care operations, role-based access, and confidential communication requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient, caregiver, payer, provider, and operations disclosure boundary handling
used_for: treatment, payment, and operations disclosure boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Eligibility Transaction System (HETS) 270/271
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS operates Medicare real-time eligibility transaction infrastructure. HETS supports 270/271 eligibility evidence and the boundary between eligibility status and service clearance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility verification and coverage evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS-0057-F is federal public policy for impacted payer prior authorization APIs, status transparency, and denial-reason communication. It constrains status handling without granting approval authority to customer care.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization status readiness and payer-response escalation

authoritative_sources.title: OASIS Data Sets
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS OASIS instruments are the public home health assessment and quality reporting artifact baseline, including OASIS-E2 effective April 1, 2026.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home health assessment status, quality reporting artifact, and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is a recognized vendor-neutral operating-rules body for healthcare administrative transactions, including eligibility, benefits, prior authorization, referrals, attachments, acknowledgements, and claim-status workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, referral, authorization, attachment, acknowledgement, and claim-status workflow

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Minimum prerequisites include confirmed patient/member identity, requester authority, service address, service date, requested support action, payer and plan context, patient or member status, referral or order status, authorization status, plan-of-care or visit-frequency context, field staff assignment, privacy and consent posture, source-of-truth hierarchy, and tenant service recovery or escalation authority.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR or EMR, home health scheduling or EVV systems, CRM and communication logs, referral and authorization platforms, payer portals, eligibility transaction systems, case management and utilization review tools, practice-management or revenue-cycle systems, document repositories, OASIS or assessment artifacts, privacy and consent systems, and audit logs. Tenant source hierarchy decides which record controls when systems conflict.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, symptom triage, treatment planning, medication guidance, plan-of-care revision, clinical urgency, medical necessity, utilization review judgment, authorization approval, payer policy interpretation, coverage determination, privacy-release approval, legal interpretation, coding, claim correction, denial or appeal strategy, refund approval, compensation, or service-recovery exception approval.

workflow_stages.name: Support intake and requester authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request type, patient or member identity, requester role, contact channel, service address, visit date, payer, plan, service model, and privacy constraints before treating the support request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Home service record and prerequisite review
workflow_stages.description: Review EHR or EMR, scheduling, EVV, CRM, referral, authorization, eligibility, plan-of-care, OASIS, case-management, utilization review, payer portal, and privacy records to classify the request as resolvable, blocked, or routed.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution and communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a service-support disposition, resolution note, and customer or stakeholder communication summary that states issue, evidence used, action taken, unresolved items, and next owner without overstating coverage, clinical, or approval authority.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, repeat-failure, and unsupported workaround escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missed visits, repeated unresolved contacts, service-recovery asks, refund or compensation requests, non-covered service requests, and policy conflicts with evidence and approval-owner routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, patient-safety, clinical, and payer boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop direct resolution when PHI, consent, representative status, patient safety, clinical judgment, payer interpretation, medical necessity, coding, claim, denial, appeal, or authorization approval changes the safe answer.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff, closure, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Record whether the request is resolved, blocked, or transferred; preserve public baseline constraints; and apply tenant-specific service model, payer, jurisdiction, field-staff, privacy, and escalation overlays only with documented assumptions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: patient or member identifiers, MRN or account number, DOB, service address, caregiver or representative record, communication preferences, consent, proxy, privacy restriction, and audit evidence
visit schedule, EVV record, field clinician or aide assignment, missed or delayed visit note, service-level record, CRM interaction, complaint record, and supervisor callback task
EHR or EMR encounter documentation, orders, plan of care, visit frequency, clinical-administrative notes, OASIS or assessment status, discharge summary, and care-transition record
eligibility and benefit responses, 270/271 evidence, payer portal status, referral order, authorization record, authorization number, ABN, utilization review note, and case-management task
practice-management, revenue-cycle, coding, charge capture, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal documentation when used only to route work to adjacent owners

resolution notes, missing-prerequisite requests, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, exception communication escalation notes, adjacent-specialist handoffs, and low-confidence escalation notes
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
home health scheduling, dispatch, field-service, or EVV systems
CRM, contact-center, portal messaging, and communication-log systems
practice management, revenue cycle, billing, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
payer portals and electronic prior-authorization platforms
referral management and authorization management systems
case management and utilization review systems
OASIS, assessment, quality reporting, and document repository systems
consent, privacy, identity-access, and audit systems

allowed: Classify customer-care requests as informational, in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, exception-driven, complaint-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Explain documented service, visit, referral, authorization, eligibility, or care-transition status using authoritative records and approved communication rules.
Prepare resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, request-resolution summaries, missing-prerequisite requests, and escalation packets with source traceability.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source-of-truth hierarchy, jurisdiction, service model, patient/member status, payer, plan, authorization, referral, order, visit, privacy, consent, or field-owner context is missing and would change the disposition.
EHR, scheduling, EVV, payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, CRM, case-management, utilization review, privacy, or revenue-cycle records materially conflict.

The request involves PHI disclosure uncertainty, representative authority ambiguity, patient-safety risk, clinical risk, medical necessity, payer policy interpretation, authorization approval, coding, claim, denial, appeal, refund, compensation, or service-recovery approval.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy, payer contract, state program, facility or agency rule, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflict.
refuse: Resolve a service request when required tenant facts, records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Diagnose, triage symptoms, prescribe treatment, change visit frequency as a clinical matter, revise a plan of care, advise on medication, or imply licensed clinical judgment.
Approve or deny authorization, coverage, medical necessity, payer policy interpretation, coding, claim correction, denial, appeal, refund, compensation, or service recovery.
Disclose PHI when requester authority, consent, proxy status, minimum-necessary purpose, or confidential communication rule is unresolved.
Override policy, pricing, eligibility, referral, authorization, visit, or documentation limits to close a case.

domain_failure_modes: customer care schedules or promises a start of care when referral, order, authorization, eligibility, or documentation prerequisites are missing
the lane treats a clinical symptom, medication, treatment, or plan-of-care question as routine customer service
PHI is disclosed to a caregiver or family member without verified authority or minimum-necessary controls
active eligibility is converted into a final coverage, authorization, or medical necessity conclusion
missed visits or repeat failures are closed as ordinary status updates without patient-safety or service-recovery escalation
EHR, scheduling, EVV, payer portal, referral, authorization, and CRM records conflict and customer care guesses a final answer
billing, coding, claim, denial, or appeal work is handled in-lane instead of routed to revenue-cycle or coding ownership
tenant-specific private-duty or service-recovery rules are used to weaken public healthcare, privacy, or payer boundaries
the industry label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic customer-support scripting without home health terminology, artifacts, or escalation logic

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The overlay family is home services and field consumer services while the resolved domain research pack is healthcare and care delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package constrains the lane to customer-facing home health, home care, and medically adjacent in-home service support. Runtime tenants must supply whether a program is Medicare home health, Medicaid waiver, private-duty home care, hospice-adjacent, or non-medical field service because that determines payer, privacy, clinical, and field-staff boundaries.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State Medicaid, private-duty home care, licensure, refund, service-recovery, and home care agency rules vary materially by tenant jurisdiction and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses federal public healthcare and home health sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, program type, payer contract, state rules, agency policy, and authority matrix before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, failure handling, privacy, patient safety, coverage, authorization, referral, and clinical-versus-administrative boundaries.

The lane is intentionally administrative. Clinical review, utilization management, payer operations, privacy approval, coding, billing, denials, appeals, field supervision, and service-recovery approval remain adjacent ownership.
